Potential Outcome of the Deal

Themay look to add pitching depth before the 2026 season begins. One name that has surfaced in trade discussion isThe conversation comes during spring training as teams evaluate their rosters before Opening Day. Lauer finished the 2025 season strongly, and Toronto currently has several rotation options. That situation has created speculation that the Blue Jays could move him in a deal that helps both teams.One suggested trade would send Lauer to Los Angeles Dodgers. In return, the Dodgers would trade shortstop prospect Kellon Lindsey and right-handed pitcher Brooks Auger to Toronto Blue Jays.Lauer is scheduled to earn $4.4 million in 2026 after losing his arbitration case.Because of the modest salary, the deal would not require cash adjustments. If both teams agree, the trade would be simple to complete before the season starts.The Dodgers expect their starting rotation to include Yoshinobu Yamamoto, Tyler Glasnow, Shohei Ohtani, Emmet Sheehan, and Roki Sasaki.Sasaki has struggled in spring training, which has created some concern about the back end of the rotation. The team also has injuries in the pitching staff.Reliever Evan Phillips is expected to miss time after Tommy John surgery, which reduces the number of pitchers who can move between starting and relief roles.Lauer could fill that gap. He has experience as both a starter and a reliever and could cover innings if one of the starters struggles or misses time.Toronto Blue Jays also have questions in their pitching staff because of injuries. Bowden Francis will miss the entire 2026 season after Tommy John surgery. Shane Bieber is being brought back carefully because of arm fatigue. Anthony Santander is expected to miss five to six months.Even with those injuries, the Blue Jays still have several pitchers who can start. José Berríos leads the rotation, and pitchers such as Dylan Cease provide additional depth. In that situation, Lauer could end up as the sixth starter or move to the bullpen.Trading him now would allow Toronto to add prospects while creating roster flexibility before he becomes a free agent.Eric Lauer, a 30-year-old left-handed pitcher, had a strong 2025 season. He appeared in 28 games and started 15 of them. He finished with a 9–2 record, a 3.18 ERA, and 102 strikeouts in 104.2 innings.He was especially effective late in the season, when he was one of Toronto’s most reliable starters during August and September.Across seven major league seasons, Eric Lauer has a 45–39 record with a 4.13 ERA. His pitch mix has also worked well against right-handed hitters, who have a .295 expected weighted on-base average against him.For the Los Angeles Dodgers, Eric Lauer would provide a left-handed pitcher who can handle several innings when needed. He would give the team another option without affecting the top of the rotation.Toronto Blue Jays would receive two younger players. Lindsey could add depth to the infield, and Auger would become another pitching prospect in the system. The move would also clear space in the rotation while bringing in future talent.Lauer could also benefit if the move gives him more opportunities to start for a team that expects to compete for a championship.If the trade happens, the Dodgers would gain another experienced pitcher who can help stabilize the staff during the season. Toronto would add prospects and adjust its rotation before the year begins.Also read: MLB trade rumors: New York Yankees could sign $330 million Philadelphia Phillies franchise superstar to form power duo with Aaron Judge The discussion remains speculative, but it reflects how both teams could address roster needs before Opening Day in 2026.
Article Summary: Eric lauer, a 30-year-old left-handed pitcher, had a strong 2025 season. He appeared in 28 games and started 15 of them. Lauer is scheduled to earn $4.4 million in 2026 after losing his arbitration case. The conversation comes during spring training as teams evaluate their rosters.

Article Title: Why the 'win the semifinal, win the trophy' trend matters in India vs England clash
Article Text:
Suryakumar Yadav and Harry Brook (Image credit: Agencies)

India arrive for final net session before T20 World Cup semifinal

Probable playing XIs:

India

England

India and England have each lifted the T20 World Cup twice, and their rivalry in the semifinals has been perfectly balanced so far — one win apiece. Interestingly, on both occasions, the team that won the semifinal went on to clinch the title.Now, as the two heavyweights prepare for their third semifinal meeting in three editions at Mumbai’s iconic Wankhede Stadium on Thursday, history beckons. The winner will have a shot at becoming the first nation to claim the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup title three times. Alongside India and England, only the West Indies boast two trophies.Also See: SA vs NZ Live Score T20 World Cup 2026 Semifinal England triumphed in the 2022 semifinal, crushing India by 10 wickets before defeating Pakistan in the final.India returned the favour in 2024, overcoming England in the last four and then edging South Africa in a tense summit clash to secure their second crown.Who prevails this time? While conditions and numbers may slightly favour India, England’s big-match temperament makes them equally formidable.Overall, the two sides have met 29 times in T20Is, with India winning 17 and England 12. India are chasing further milestones — becoming the first team to defend a T20 World Cup title and the first host nation to lift the trophy.Their contests have rarely lacked intensity, and Thursday promises to be no different, with both teams expected to go all out.The journeys to the semifinals have been contrasting. England were the first to qualify, finishing unbeaten in Group 2 of the Super 8s with five consecutive wins. India, meanwhile, suffered a defeat to South Africa and only sealed their spot with a dramatic must-win victory over the West Indies.Earlier in the league stage, India topped Group A with a flawless record, while England finished second in Pool C after a loss to the Caribbean side.Though the semifinal will write a fresh script, memories at Wankhede add emotional weight for the hosts — from MS Dhoni’s iconic six to seal the 2011 ODI World Cup to Sachin Tendulkar’s teammates carrying him on their shoulders in celebration.India’s campaign has been defined by different match-winners stepping up at crucial moments. Abhishek Sharma struck his maiden fifty of the tournament in the Super 8s, while Sanju Samson’s unbeaten 97 in the must-win clash against West Indies proved decisive. Skipper Suryakumar Yadav set the tone early with a brilliant 84 against USA, and contributions from Tilak Varma, Ishan Kishan and Hardik Pandya have strengthened the batting unit. Suryakumar leads the scoring charts for India with 231 runs, followed by Ishan with 217.The bowling attack has been equally collective. Jasprit Bumrah, Arshdeep Singh, Varun Chakravarthy, Axar Patel, Hardik and Kuldeep Yadav have all chipped in with key breakthroughs, with Chakravarthy leading the wicket tally with 12 scalps. No single player has dominated consistently — but that shared responsibility has powered India’s run.England’s story mirrors that pattern. They too have relied on collective effort rather than individual brilliance. Phil Salt, Jacob Bethell, Will Jacks and captain Harry Brook have delivered vital contributions, even as Jos Buttler searches for top form. Brook tops their scoring list with 228 runs. With the ball, Adil Rashid has been their standout performer, claiming 11 wickets so far.The Wankhede pitch is expected to assist seamers early before settling into a batting-friendly surface. With hot and sunny conditions forecast and dew likely later in the evening, the toss could play a role, tempting captains to bowl first.Both teams appear settled after seven matches and are unlikely to tinker with their combinations. For India, that means another opportunity for Sanju Samson to build on his momentum.With over 35,000 fans expected to pack the stands, Wankhede will be a cauldron of noise. Under the lights, in front of a roaring home crowd, the side that holds its nerve will march into the final in Ahmedabad.: Sanju Samson (wk), Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan, Suryakumar Yadav (c), Tilak Varma, Hardik Pandya, Shivam Dube, Axar Patel, Arshdeep Singh, Varun Chakaravarthy, Jasprit Bumrah: Phil Salt, Jos Buttler (wk), Harry Brook (c), Jacob Bethell, Tom Banton, Sam Curran, Will Jacks, Liam Dawson, Rehan Ahmed, Jofra Archer, Adil Rashid.
Article Summary: India and england have met 29 times in t20is, with india winning 17 and england 12. The winner will have a shot at becoming the first nation to claim the icc men’s t20 world cup title three times. Both teams appear settled after seven matches and are unlikely to tinker with their combinations.

Article Title: Is USA Hockey star Jack Hughes dating Canadian pop star Tate McRae? All about the duo’s current relationship status
Article Text:
Jack Hughes and Tate McRae. Image via: Getty Images

Are Jack Hughes and Tate McRae dating? What we know so far



How did Jack Hughes and Tate McRae's romance begin?



Jack Hughes is riding high after delivering Olympic gold for Team USA. Tate McRae continues to dominate global charts with back-to-back hits. Now, their personal lives have collided in a way fans did not expect. After months of speculation, sources have confirmed that the USA Hockey standout and the Canadian pop star are officially seeing each other.The rumors began quietly in New York City. They created a buzz after arena sightings and social media traction. The rumor started as whispers and has now turned into confirmed reports that the two young stars are building something new while balancing demanding careers on ice and on stage.Yes. According to Us Weekly, the relationship is real.“Tate and Jack are dating,” a source confirmed to "Us Weekly." “They started casually seeing each other late last year, so it’s still new, but they are exclusively seeing each other.”The 24-year-old Hughes, who plays for the New Jersey Devils, reportedly made the first move. The outlet states that he “initially messaged her on IG and started a conversation.” His message opened the door to what has now become an exclusive relationship.“She thinks he is a really cool guy and they have been having a lot of fun together,” the insider said, adding, “Tate thinks he’s really sweet and it’s going well so far.”McRae has been spotted at Devils games, cheering from the stands. “She has been so supportive of his career and has been loving going to the games and cheering him on,” the source continued. “They are making it work during this busy season, but hope to have more time together next month when his season ends.”The pair were first linked in December 2025 after being seen together in New York following a Devils game at Newark’s Prudential Center. They have not publicly confirmed the relationship.The story began in direct messages. Hughes reached out on Instagram. From there, casual meetings became more consistent.Hughes had recently helped Team USA secure Olympic gold in Milan, elevating his profile beyond the NHL . McRae, 22, was fresh off her Miss Possessive Tour and continued promoting chart-topping singles like “Greedy.” Their schedules remain packed, yet sources say they have found time to connect.This is not McRae’s first link to the hockey world. She previously dated NHL forward Cole Sillinger and was also in a high-profile relationship with The Kid LAROI.
Article Summary: Jack hughes and tate mcrae are dating, a source confirmed to "us weekly" the pair were first linked in december after being seen together in new york. The 24-year-old new jersey devils player reportedly made the first move. Mcrae, 22, was fresh off her miss possessive tour.

Article Title: “It was pretty hilarious” - Draymond Green breaks his silence on Stephen Curry’s viral reaction to former’s speech during Los Angeles Lakers clash
Article Text:
Stephen Curry and Draymond Green (Image via Getty)

Draymond Green clears air on Stephen Curry’s reaction to his speech during Golden State Warriors vs Los Angeles Lakers timeout



Who will the Golden State Warriors face next?

On 28 February, the Golden State Warriors faced the Los Angeles Lakers. During the timeout of that game, a clip went viral. It featured Draymond Green giving a speech to the rest of the teammates and Stephen Curry looking at Green. The look was interpreted by fans as being done with Draymond. Reacting to these interpretations, Draymond broke his silence recently. He talked about what he understood from watching the clip.Draymond emphasized the kind of relationship shared with Stephen. He believed that, in reality, people were trying to project their perception of Draymond in the name of Stephen. Draymond rather found the interpretations pretty hilarious.The Warriors athlete cleared the air in the recent episode of The Draymond Green Show.Both Draymond Green and Stephen Curry have been a part of the Golden State Warriors since the beginning of their respective careers. Draymond was drafted in 2012 and Stephen was drafted in 2009. This long period of playing for the team and as teammates definitely resulted in a strong bond. Draymond reflected on this bond while breaking his silence on the recent viral clip and its interpretations.The Warriors athlete referred to the viral clip and stated, “I see a lot of people’s reactions, like, 'Oh man, Steph’s tired of Draymond. ’ And quite honestly, what I saw in the video and what I’ve taken away from the video and be that I kind of talk to Steph everyday, was that he was in agreeance of what I said.”Draymond further stated, “We were getting our ass kicked, so you’re not going to be sitting there smiling or enthused. And yeah, I love how everyone tries to project their energy on about me onto Steph. When, quite honestly, their energy really doesn’t f*cking matter when it comes to me.” He continued, “So I really enjoy seeing all the takes online and everybody be like, ‘Oh man he is so sick of Draymond, I am so sick of Draymond. ’ The reality is you being sick of me, you can either not watch or you can shut the hell up. Because it don’t matter. I think the reaction to it was pretty hilarious." It is quite clear now how Green has interpreted the opinions about the viral clip online.The Golden State Warriors will face the Houston Rockets next. They previously faced the LA Clippers on March 2. The Warriors lost that game with a 13-point deficit. Now, the team is gearing up to clash against the Rockets. Scheduled for March 5, the game will be held at the Toyota Center, Houston.
Article Summary: Draymond green gave a speech during golden state warriors vs los angeles lakers timeout. Stephen curry looked at draymond in a way that was interpreted as being done with him. Green broke his silence on the viral clip and its interpretations. The warriors will face the houston rockets next.

Article Title: Who is Auston Matthews’ new girlfriend Vanessa? Why it's a good news for Maple Leafs fans
Article Text:
Auston Matthews’ new girlfriend Vanessa (Instagram)

Auston Matthews’ new girlfriend Vanessa Piunno : Who she is and how their relationship went public

Auston Matthews finally has his Olympic gold, and the noise that once followed him in Toronto feels quieter now. Auston Matthews delivered when it mattered most at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Games, scoring three goals and adding four assists to power Team USA’s attack. When the final horn sounded after overtime against Canada, the weight he had carried for years seemed to lift.For a player often judged by what he has not won with the Toronto Maple Leafs, this tournament offered clarity. Matthews was central to everything the Americans did well. He drove play, created space, and produced in big moments. And when Jack Hughes buried the overtime winner against Canada men's national ice hockey team, securing the program’s first Olympic gold since 1980, Matthews’ fingerprints were all over the run.An, now Matthews has a new girlfriend, Vanessa.The celebration did not end on the ice. Days after the gold medal game, Matthews quietly confirmed another change in his life. On March 3, Vanessa Piunno shared a series of intimate photos on Instagram, wrapping her arms around the Leafs captain in Milan as he wore his gold medal. Her caption read, "A month to remember forever ❤️❤️," and it left little room for doubt.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.Piunno is Toronto based and runs her own skincare company, Piunno Beauty. According to her website, "Today, I offer private 1:1 skincare trainings and courses designed for everyone. Whether you’re a complete beginner, a skincare enthusiast, or someone looking to deepen your knowledge and confidence. My approachis personal, thoughtful, and rooted in education. I believe skincare should feel empowering, not intimidating, andthat learning works best when it’s tailored to you. "She has since given followers small glimpses of their life away from the rink. One Instagram Story showed a bowl of seasoned braised beef. Another revealed the finished dish, sweet potato braised beef gnocchi topped with shredded cheese. It was simple, warm, and personal. A reminder that even elite athletes return home to ordinary moments.The timing drew attention. Matthews had previously been linked to Emily Ruttledge, a licensed therapist based in Santa Monica. When there were no public congratulations during the Olympic run, speculation grew. Piunno’s post effectively confirmed a new chapter.Matthews remains private by nature. He rarely shares details beyond hockey. Yet this week felt different. With a gold medal secured and his contract keeping him in Toronto for the next four years, stability seems easier to find. On the ice, he answered critics who questioned whether he could lead a champion. Off it, he appears settled.Gold medals tend to silence debates. They also mark turning points. For Matthews, Milano Cortina delivered both.
Article Summary: Auston matthews scored three goals and added four assists to power team usa’s attack. Days after the gold medal game, matthews quietly confirmed another change in his life. On march 3, vanessa piunno shared a series of intimate photos on instagram.

Article Title: Aryna Sabalenka engaged: All about Grand Slam champion's 12-carat diamond engagement ring from Georgios Frangulis
Article Text:
Aryna Sabalenka engaged. Image via: Aryna Sabalenka/ Instagram | Mike Frey-Imagn Images

Aryna Sabalenka flaunts a 12-carat diamond engagement ring from Georgios Frangulis



Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis receive warm wishes



Aryna Sabalenka’s luxury getaway with Georgios Frangulis sparks buzz as Nick Kyrgios reacts to her watch after Wimbledon heartbreak (Image Via X)

Wedding bells are ringing for world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka. The Grand Slam champion said yes to Brazilian entrepreneur Georgios Frangulis after a surprise proposal that unfolded beside a candlelit pool framed with pink roses. The moment, shared on Instagram, quickly drew global attention. Yet it was the ring that sparked a second wave of headlines.Sabalenka’s engagement announcement comes during a busy stretch in her career, as she competes at Indian Wells in California. Of course, Sabalenka’s focus is still on the court; her personal milestone has captured fans across the tennis world, drawing warm messages from fellow athletes and celebrities.And she deserves the little moment of joy and personal celebration.The custom piece features a stunning oval cut diamond weighing over 12 carats, set in platinum with emerald accents. Emeralds hold special meaning for Sabalenka. They are her favorite stone, and the idea to include them came from Frangulis himself.Isabela Grutman, CEO and Founder of ISA Grutman Jewelry, worked closely with him on the design. “I've always admired their love and relationship, so I was incredibly flattered when Georgios asked me to design this piece,” Grutman said in a statement (via Daily Mail).“We spent months working on the design, selecting the stones, and perfecting every detail of the craftsmanship to make it truly special for Aryna,” she continued. “What made it even more meaningful was Georgios' idea to incorporate emeralds into the design, as it's her favorite stone - a personal touch that makes the ring uniquely hers.”Jewelry experts estimate the ring’s value could range between $500,000 and $800,000. Its elongated oval shape enhances its presence on the finger, a trend that has grown popular in recent years.Sabalenka later admitted she did not expect the proposal that night. “Well, I obviously had NO idea this was happening tonight,” she wrote alongside a close-up video of the sparkling diamond.The couple’s announcement prompted congratulations from across the sports world, including Novak Djokovic, Coco Gauff, and other tennis stars. Their relationship, first linked publicly in April 2024, has steadily grown in the spotlight.Sabalenka had hinted at marriage before. During the Brisbane International, she smiled and said, “Thank you to my boyfriend. Hopefully soon I'll call you somehow else, right?” She then added with a laugh, “I just put on extra pressure, right?”Now engaged, Sabalenka begins a new chapter with Frangulis.
Article Summary: Tennis star aryna sabalenka is engaged to brazilian entrepreneur georgios frangulis. The 12-carat diamond engagement ring is set in platinum with emerald accents. The custom piece features a stunning oval cut diamond weighing over 12 carats. The couple's relationship has steadily grown in the spotlight.

Article Title: T20 World Cup 2026 SA vs NZ semifinal: What happens if South Africa vs New Zealand match is washed out in Kolkata?
Article Text:
South Africa, unbeaten in the tournament so far, will look to continue their quest for a maiden World Cup title when they take on New Zealand. (ICC Photo)

Indian cricket team leaves from Kolkata, fans go mad for Sanju Samson

T20 World Cup semifinal, SA vs NZ : What happens if rain washes out match?



T20 World Cup semifinal, SA vs NZ: Weather Forecast in Kolkata

T20 World Cup semifinal, SA vs NZ: Squads

NEW DELHI: New Zealand and South Africa are set to lock horns for a coveted place in the final when they meet in the first semi-final of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata on Wednesday.The rivalry between the two sides has produced some unforgettable moments, none more dramatic than the 2015 ODI World Cup semi-final. On that occasion, New Zealand clinched a thrilling last-over victory, a result that left South Africa shattered and further cemented their reputation for stumbling at crucial stages.This Proteas side, however, carries a different aura. Guided by coach Shukri Conrad, South Africa have transformed into a mentally tougher and more composed outfit.Their World Test Championship triumph last year signalled a shift in belief and temperament. Though a major white-ball title still eludes them, the long-standing “chokers” narrative no longer hangs as heavily over the team.Much of the spotlight will fall on the two captains, both of whom have led from the front. Aiden Markram has been in destructive form, scoring 268 runs at a strike rate exceeding 175 and frequently seizing control during the powerplay.Mitchell Santner, meanwhile, has been equally influential in a different role — applying the brakes with the ball, boasting an impressive economy rate of 6.35 and consistently delivering in key phases.South Africa’s batting unit looks formidable on paper. Alongside Markram, the likes of Quinton de Kock, Dewald Brevis, Ryan Rickelton, Tristan Stubbs, David Miller and Marco Jansen provide depth, versatility and explosive finishing power. On a good batting surface, this line-up has the potential to overwhelm opposition attacks.New Zealand, though, bring balance and discipline, particularly through their spin department. Santner has marshalled his resources smartly, with Rachin Ravindra (nine wickets at an economy under seven), Glenn Phillips and Cole McConchie offering control and breakthroughs. Barring India, this may be the most effective spin combination South Africa have faced in the tournament.One possible weakness for the Black Caps is the lack of a consistent wrist-spin threat. Ish Sodhi has yet to make a significant impact in his appearances, which could leave New Zealand slightly short in that department.The Eden Gardens surface is expected to assist batters, raising the prospect of a high-scoring contest. South Africa might prefer chasing, especially since New Zealand’s Super Eight fixtures were played on slower pitches in Sri Lanka. On a truer Kolkata track, aggressive stroke-makers such as Finn Allen, Tim Seifert, Glenn Phillips and Daryl Mitchell could aim for a total in excess of 200.With the ball, Lungi Ngidi’s variations — including his slower deliveries, wide yorkers and leg-cutters — may be key for South Africa on a flat pitch. For New Zealand, much could hinge on Lockie Ferguson’s opening spell, which has the potential to set the tone in a high-stakes encounter.Weather interruptions remain a talking point in Kolkata.If rain halts play on Wednesday (March 4), the match will not be abandoned immediately. Tournament regulations include a reserve day to ensure a result is achieved. The game would resume from the exact point it was stopped, preserving fairness.March 5 (Thursday) has been designated as the official reserve day.If both days are completely washed out, the team that finished higher in the Super Eight stage will advance to the final.South Africa topped Super 8 Group 1 with six points, while New Zealand qualified from Group 2 with three points.As a result, if the semi-final is completely washed out on both the scheduled day and the reserve day, South Africa would progress to the final by virtue of their superior Super Eight standing.According to the latest forecast, Kolkata is expected to enjoy sunny conditions on March 4, with no rain or thunderstorms predicted. Temperatures are likely to peak at around 33°C, and the chance of precipitation remains minimal — making a washout appear unlikely for this crucial semi-final showdown.South Africa Squad: Aiden Markram(c), Quinton de Kock(w), Ryan Rickelton, Dewald Brevis, David Miller, Tristan Stubbs, Marco Jansen, Corbin Bosch, Kagiso Rabada, Keshav Maharaj, Lungi Ngidi, George Linde, Anrich Nortje, Kwena Maphaka, Jason SmithNew Zealand Squad: Tim Seifert(w), Finn Allen, Rachin Ravindra, Glenn Phillips, Daryl Mitchell, Mark Chapman, Mitchell Santner(c), Cole McConchie, Matt Henry, Ish Sodhi, Lockie Ferguson, James Neesham, Jacob Duffy, Kyle Jamieson, Devon Conway
Article Summary: New zealand and south africa will meet in the first semi-final of the icc men's t20 world cup 2026 at the eden gardens in kolkata on wednesday. The rivalry between the two sides has produced some unforgettable moments. South africa, unbeaten in the tournament so far, will look to continue their quest for a maiden world cup title.

Article Title: Mohammad Amir: India T20 World Cup final nahi khelega
Article Text:
BCCI Photo

Team India arrives in Mumbai after sealing semifinal spot | T20 World Cup 2026

SAMSON MASTERCLASS

SAMSON SURPASSES KOHLI

NEW DELHI:Former Pakistan pacer Mohammad Amir has found himself at the centre of social media criticism after his bold prediction about India backfired. Amir had earlier claimed that India would fail to reach the semifinals of the T20 World Cup. However, India silenced doubters with a commanding five-wicket win over the West Indies in their virtual quarterfinal at Eden Gardens in Kolkata on Sunday. Powered by Sanju Samson’s unbeaten 97, India sealed a semifinal berth in style.But Amir has now reignited debate with another strong statement, asserting that India still won’t make the final."India complete cricket nahi khel rha. India final nahi kehelega ]India are not playing complete cricket.India will not play the final]," Amir said.Elaborating further, he said: "My semifinal analysis was correct, but Sanju Samson played really good cricket. Still, I feel India is not playing complete cricket yet, apart from Jasprit Bumrah , not a single bowler is in form. Just look at their batting, in almost every match, they keep suffering collapses. The semifinal against England will be a good contest, but I am sure India will not play the final."After winning the toss, India opted to bowl first. West Indies openers Roston Chase (40 off 25, five fours and a six) and captain Shai Hope (32 off 33, three fours and a six) gave their side a solid start with a 68-run opening partnership. However, the innings lost momentum as they slipped to 119/4 in 14.1 overs, despite a quickfire 27 off 12 balls from Shimron Hetmyer.A late surge from Jason Holder (37* off 22, two fours and three sixes) and Rovman Powell (34* off 19, three fours and two sixes) saw the pair add 76 runs in just 35 balls, pushing West Indies to 195/4 in 20 overs.Jasprit Bumrah (2/36) was India’s most effective bowler. Varun Chakaravarthy and Hardik Pandya picked up one wicket each, conceding 40 runs apiece in their four overs.Chasing 196, India stumbled early and were 41/2 inside the powerplay. Samson steadied the innings with a 58-run stand alongside Suryakumar Yadav (18). He then built crucial partnerships with Tilak Varma (27 off 15), Hardik Pandya (17) and Shivam Dube (8*), guiding India to victory with five wickets in hand.Samson’s 97* is now the highest individual score by an Indian in a successful T20 World Cup run-chase. He surpassed Virat Kohli’s unbeaten 82 against Australia in Mohali (2016) and Pakistan in Melbourne (2022).Across the IPL and T20Is, Samson has opened the batting 29 times in run-chases, and this was the first time he remained unbeaten till the end.The chase of 196 is India’s highest in T20 World Cup history, eclipsing their 173-run chase against South Africa in Mirpur in 2014. With the win, India secured a record-equalling sixth semifinal appearance in T20 World Cup history, matching Pakistan (2007, 2009, 2010, 2012, 2021, 2022) and England (2010, 2016, 2021, 2022, 2024, 2026).Samson’s knock is also the second-highest individual score by an Indian in T20 World Cup history, behind Suresh Raina’s 101 against South Africa in Gros Islet in 2010.
Article Summary: India beat west indies by five wickets in t20 world cup quarterfinal. Sanju samson's unbeaten 97 powered india to victory in kolkata. Former pakistan pacer mohammad amir claims india are not playing 'complete cricket' india will play england in the semifinals in mumbai on wednesday.

Article Title: Aiden Markram adds calm to the proceedings: ‘Locked-in’
Article Text:
South Africa's captain Aiden Markram, right, and Kagiso Rabada. (AP Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

T20 World Cup: Shukri Conrad press conference after SA vs ZIM

NEW DELHI: Nineteen months ago, Aiden Markram appeared dazed on the boundary ropes as India celebrated their second T20 World title in Bridgetown, Barbados. The South African captain later admitted he was ‘gutted’ that his side couldn’t get over the finish line despite appearing to be in control of their chase of 177 in the final.Cut to Lord’s, June 2025. Playing under Temba Bavuma , Markram was beaming from ear to ear and even found time to enjoy a glass of beer near the boundary fence with an old friend after guiding South Africa to their maiden world title with a scintillating 136 against Australia in the World Test Championship final.Having led the South African side to a World Cup title in the U-19 World Cup in 2014, the 31-year-old has faced success and disappointments in equal measure in his eight-year international career.Having assumed the full-time captaincy responsibilities of the SA T20 side in 2023, Markram appears calmer and more composed. But beyond the ice-cool demeanour lies a fierce competitor who commands the respect of the entire unit.“Aiden has been fantastic both with bat in hand and as a leader. But you’re only as good as your troops and he would be the first one to admit that. So, it’s easy to captain a bowling unit that’s on top of their game and batters that are firing.But Aiden, yeah, he’s been at the forefront of everything we do,” South African head coach Shukri Conrad said.SA batting coach Ashwell Prince indicated that the skipper often acted as the bridge between the players and the support staff. “When he steps on to the field, he has a clear idea about all the discussions that we’ve had and the different possibilities that could arise during the match. In terms of his leadership, he’s got great communication with these bowlers out there in the middle, with the coaching staff. We tend to just trust him with all the ideas and information,” Prince said.“I’ve always been of the opinion that opening is his best position. The phrase that my kids would use is he’s really ‘locked-in’ at the moment in terms of his focus,” Prince added.
Article Summary: Aiden markram will captain south africa at the 2023 t20 world cup. The 31-year-old guided sa to their maiden world title with 136 against australia. Markram has faced success and disappointments in equal measure in his eight-year international career.

Article Title: Determined South Africa look to add cherry on the ‘cupcake’ at T20 World Cup
Article Text:
South Africa's captain Aiden Markram with teammates. (PTI Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

T20 World Cup: Shukri Conrad press conference after SA vs ZIM

NEW DELHI: The South African cricket team is in familiar terrain, having reached the semifinals of yet another World Cup. There’s nothing new about this — they’ve reached this stage in an ICC World Cup (T20 and ODI World Cups combined) on eight previous occasions.Though they won the World Test Championship (WTC) title last year to break the jinx, the South Africans haven’t been able to crack the code just yet in limited-overs World Cups.They have entered the final only once, where they lost to India in the previous edition of the T20 World Cup in the Caribbean. Their inability to handle pressure in knockout games in big limited-overs tournaments is forever a talking point and have given them the tag of ‘chokers’.When reminded him of that unwarranted tag after his team’s five-wicket win over Zimbabwe in their last Super 8 engagement, South Africa’s head coach Shukri Conrad not only refused to utter the word ‘chokers’ but also took the opportunity to take at a jibe at the ‘cupcake’ commercial aired by the tournament’s official broadcasters before their game against India last week.“As for the ‘C’ word, I think there is another one for it, cupcakes! I thought we kind of enjoyed that,” he said in a tongue-in-cheek manner, referring to the ad — showing a South African choking on a cupcake and then being handed a bottle of water — that was later taken off air.However, the way his team has been playing, Conrad will know that this tournament presents them with the ideal opportunity to end their search for a world title in the white-ball format.South Africa have shown that they can not only win in clinical fashion but also scramble through. While the victories over bigger teams like India, New Zealand and West Indies were comprehensive, they also managed to pip Afghanistan in a nerve-wracking Super Over and were able to scrape through against Zimbabwe, the two games in which the South Africans were clearly not at their best.“That’s the mark of really good sides. When things get tough, they’re still able to fashion something and eke out the right result. I think there’s that calmness in the side, especially in the batting unit, that whatever the situation, someone will find a way,” said Conrad.While the batsmen have done their part, it’s the performance of the South African pacers on Indian wickets that have been a revelation. “You can only pretty much cut your coat according to your cloth. Something South African cricket has always had is fast bowlers. We’re talking about guys that can bowl 145-plus. What’s going to be important in India is if you can nail a yorker, some change-ups and maybe on wickets that might be a little bit iffy, some good pace can do the trick. It’s kind of worked out for us,” Conrad said.With the batting and bowling units clicking together, South Africa remain the only unbeaten side in the tournament and that makes them favourites going into the semifinal against New Zealand in Kolkata on Wednesday.
Article Summary: South africa have reached the semifinals of yet another t20 world cup. They have reached this stage in an icc world cup (t20 and odi world cups combined) on eight previous occasions. Their inability to handle pressure in knockout games in big limited-overs tournaments is forever a talking point and have given them the tag of 'chokers'

Article Title: Neeraj Phogat: BFI selection criteria questioned after female boxers allege favouritism
Article Text:
File picture of Neeraj Phogat (PTI Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Jay Shah: Kapil Dev deserves more credit for 1983 World Cup triumph

NEW DELHI: The Boxing Federation of India’s (BFI) selection criteria to pick the national team for the forthcoming Asian Championships in Ulaanbaatar on Monday ran into a controversy after two leading female boxers, Neeraj Phogat and Jyoti Gulia, alleged favouritism and manipulation of results in the selection of the 20-member contingent for the continental meet, scheduled from March 28 to April 11 in the Mongolian capital.As per the BFI’s policy, the finalists at the Asian meet in approved weight categories will be selected as India’s direct entries for the Glasgow CWG and Aichi-Nagoya Asian Games , to be held later this year.Both Neeraj and Jyoti missed out on selection for the Ulaanbaatar meet after failing a month-long evaluation test conducted by the BFI. The federation discontinued the practice of holding an open selection trial for boxers, as had been the norm, and instead introduced a performance-based assessment process, where the BFI officials and coaches have a final say.Former Youth World champion Jyoti finished third in rankings behind Nikhat Zareen and Nitu Ghanghas in the 51kg category. World Boxing Cup (WBC) Finals’ bronze winner Neeraj lost her place in the national camp after finishing outside the ranking bracket in her 65kg weight division. The four boxers selected in her weight class were Ankushita Boro, Sneha, Pranjal Yadav and Kajal.“While certain procedures may appear compliant with BFI guidelines on paper, in practice, there is evident favouritism, manipulation, and misuse of authority.Policies are being drafted and implemented in a manner that disproportionately benefits certain preferred boxers, particularly from the Services team, thereby compromising transparency and fairness in the selection process,” Neeraj wrote in her complaint to SAI director general (DG) Hari Ranjan Rao.“I request your office to order a thorough and impartial inquiry into the above-mentioned irregularities; constitute a high-level independent committee to investigate the selection procedures and review and revoke the current evaluation process and reinstate transparent open trials,” she added.Jyoti, too, wrote a mail to Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS) CEO, N S Johal, and requested a review of assessment and trial bouts while alleging favouritism.“During the competition trials, I convincingly outperformed my opponents with a clear margin (approximately 15–20 points), which can be objectively verified through the official video recordings. Despite these performances, I was placed at Rank 3 in my weight category. This outcome has caused me serious concern, as it does not appear to reflect the performance demonstrated during both the assessment tests and the trial bouts. In the interest of fairness and justice, I request a thorough and impartial review of my trial videos, assessment scores, and evaluation criteria applied in my case,” she said.
Article Summary: Neeraj phogat and jyoti gulia alleged favouritism and manipulation of results in the selection of the 20-member contingent for the continental meet. The bfi discontinued the practice of holding an open selection trial for boxers, as had been the norm, and instead introduced a performance-based assessment process.

Article Title: T20 World Cup semifinals, IND vs ENG, SA vs NZ: From on-field umpires to match referees – Full details
Article Text:
Suryakumar Yadav and Harry Brook (Image credit: Agencies)

Team India arrives in Mumbai after sealing semifinal spot | T20 World Cup 2026

T20 World Cup Semi-Final 1: South Africa vs New Zealand



T20 World Cup Semi-Final 2: India vs England

NEW DELHI: India’s Nitin Menon has been included in the elite panel of match officials appointed for the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup semifinals, set to be played on March 4 and 5.The first semifinal will see South Africa take on New Zealand at Eden Gardens in Kolkata on March 4. Defending champions India will then face England in the second semifinal at Mumbai’s Wankhede Stadium on March 5.For the clash between South Africa and New Zealand, Richard Illingworth and Alex Wharf have been named as the on-field umpires. Illingworth had officiated South Africa’s emphatic semifinal win two years ago, when they bowled Afghanistan out for 56 and cruised to a nine-wicket victory. Wharf, meanwhile, stood in New Zealand’s group-stage win over Afghanistan in the ongoing edition.In Kolkata, Nitin Menon will perform duties as the third umpire, with Rod Tucker appointed as the fourth umpire. Javagal Srinath will serve as the match referee.The second semifinal in Mumbai between India and England will be overseen on the field by Chris Gaffaney and Allahuddien Paleker. The two teams also met at this stage in 2024, a match India won by 68 runs, with Gaffaney part of the officiating team. In the current tournament, he has stood in India’s matches against South Africa and the West Indies.Paleker has officiated two England fixtures — against the West Indies and Scotland — along with India’s win over the Netherlands.Adrian Holdstock has been assigned as the third umpire for the Mumbai semifinal, while Paul Reiffel will be the fourth umpire. Andy Pycroft will take charge as the match referee.Kolkata, Wednesday, March 4On-Field Umpires: Richard Illingworth & Alex WharfThird Umpire: Nitin MenonFourth Umpire: Rod TuckerMatch Referee: Javagal SrinathMumbai, Thursday, March 5India v EnglandOn-Field Umpires: Chris Gaffaney & Allahuddien PalekerThird Umpire: Adrian HoldstockFourth Umpire: Paul ReiffelMatch Referee: Andy Pycroft
Article Summary: India's nitin menon has been included in the elite panel of match officials appointed for the icc men's t20 world cup semifinals. The first semifinal will see south africa take on new zealand at eden gardens in kolkata on march 4. Defending champions india will then face england in the second semifinal at mumbai’s wankhede stadium on march 5.

Article Title: What is The Undertaker’s net worth in 2026? Breaking down the Deadman’s WWE salary, ventures, and assets
Article Text:
The Undertaker (Image Source: Getty)

The Undertaker's WWE salary and continuing revenue streams

Real estate, lifestyle assets and long-term financial planning

The Undertaker, known around the world as Mark William Calaway, still stands tall as one of the lasting icons of the ring. Over five years since he last suited up inside the ring, the Deadman still draws financial prowess from decades of in-ring domination within WWE. Currently in 2026, The Undertaker is believed to have a net worth estimated in the region of $17 million, due to a combination of wrestling salary, long-term royalty streams, media appearances, and wise investments.Calaway's competitive days ended in 2020, but his tenure with WWE lives on. The Hall of Famer still reaps the rewards of the brand he promoted for more than three decades, appearing for but not limited to, special appearances, licensing deals and ambassadorial roles.His financial legacy is based on consistency and longevity, in addition to wise outside-the-ring diversification.Mark William Calaway apparently still receives a huge annual payout under something akin to a WWE Legends deal, even in his retirement. Industry value figures that he gets closer to $2.5 million a year based largely on promotional appearances, representation, and special programming associated with big events like WrestleManias weekends and anniversary shows.He also earns a lot of money from merchandise royalties. Although The Undertaker is a huge legacy superstar for WWE that still continues to sell in the way of branded apparel, replica championship belts, collectibles, and action figures. He is also a regular character in WWE video game franchises, providing even more royalty money.Outside of WWE, Calaway has branched into acting and live appearances. He is still thorough with his personality to make money from podcast features to fan conventions and speaking engagements. A handful of select brand deals and endorsements rooted in wrestling nostalgia add to his yearly take-home for good measure.The Undertaker has a strong portfolio of potential real estate. Calaway owns real estate throughout Texas, including a large ranch estate in the greater Austin region. These are lifestyle assets and four of them are appreciating assets and they all enhance his net worth narrative.He's also known to keep a range of cars, particularly luxury and custom cars that reflect his biker-phase mindset. Some of which are not listed, and also goes to his valuation of net worth.Calaway has kept a close watch on his money over the years, staying clear of major business gambles and instead concentrating on steady revenue streams. By being financially disciplined, he has been able to hold on to his wealth well into retirement, despite decades of elite profits during WWE's prime boom periods.
Article Summary: The undertaker, known around the world as mark william calaway, still stands tall as one of the lasting icons of the ring. Currently in 2026, the undertaker is believed to have a net worth estimated in the region of $17 million, due to a combination of wrestling salary, long-term royalty streams, media appearances, and wise investments.

Article Title: NHL Injury Update: Tampa Bay Lightning to play without forwards Dominic James and Gage Goncalves for the next two games
Article Text:
Tampa Bay Lightning's Gage Goncalves (93) and Carolina Hurricanes' K'Andre Miller (19) chase after the puck during the third period of an NHL hockey game (Image via AP Photo/Karl DeBlaker)





Tampa Bay Lightning to play without forwards Dominic James and Gage Goncalves after sustaining injuries

The Tampa Bay Lightning will play at least the next two games without forwards Dominic James and Gage Goncalves, who suffered injuries in Saturday's loss to the Buffalo Sabres, further depleting a lineup already battling through a lot of injuries this season. Head coach Jon Cooper confirmed on Monday that both players will be sidelined for Tampa Bay’s four-game road trip, ruling them out for upcoming clashes against the Minnesota Wild and Winnipeg Jets.As per reports, Dominic James suffered a leg injury when cross-checked into the boards by Sabres defenseman Michael Kesselring. Along with him, Goncalves suffered a lower-body injury during a match against the Buffalo Sabres.Both of them are expected to miss the next couple of games while the Lightning evaluates their condition currently. The Tampa Bay Lightning forwards Dominic James and Gage Goncalves will be out for at least their next two games after they both suffered injuries in Saturday’s 6-2 loss to the Buffalo Sabres. As per Yahoo Sports, rookie forward Dominic James left Saturday’s game against the Buffalo Sabres after suffering an apparent right-leg injury late in the third period.According to reports, Dominic James left the game after an apparent leg injury when he was cross-checked into the boards by Sabres defenseman Michael Kesselring. Following the incident, head coach Jon Cooper did not have an update on James’ status after the game.While Goncalves appeared to suffer his own lower-body injury after Sabres defenseman Rasmus Dahlin knocked him down with a cross-check, causing him to slide feet-first into the boards. Owing to this situation, both players will remain out from Tampa Bay’s upcoming road games, including Tuesday’s matchup against the Minnesota Wild.This season, Gage has seven goals and 13 assists for 20 points. Last season, he had eight goals and 12 assists for 20 points in 60 games played with the Tampa Bay Lightning, and James has seven goals and eight assists for 15 points in 43 games played so far.As reported by Daily Faceoff, if James and Goncalves miss only the Lightning’s next two games, the two players could be seen on Saturday, March 7th’s matchup against the Toronto Maple Leafs. Meanwhile, the Lightning are sitting atop the Atlantic Division with 80 points in 58 games.Tampa Bay will monitor both James and Goncalves’ health status closely as they recover, missing these two important matches on Tampa Bay’s four-game road trip.
Article Summary: Tampa bay lightning forwards dominic james and gage goncalves will be out for at least their next two games after they both suffered injuries in saturday’s 6-2 loss to the buffalo sabres. As per reports, dominic james suffered a leg injury when cross-checked into the boards by sabres defenseman michael kesselring.

Article Title: What is Nazem Kadri's net worth in 2026? A look at Calgary Flames star contracts and earnings
Article Text:
NHL-Nazem Kadri of the Calgary Flames (Credit: Getty Images)

Nazem Kadri: 1,000 Games In The NHL

NHL star Nazem Kadri of the Calgary Flames' salary details and career achievements are explained



Nazem Kadri has been gradually emerging as a prominent player in the NHL due to scoring capacity and diversity as a center. Kadri has been a crucial portion of the Flames' lineup since joining the Calgary Flames in 2022 due to his leadership and offensive production on the team. His ability and experience ensure that he can be a dependable individual in any NHL game.Kadri's financial success has been achieved over the years due to the stability in its operations. By 2026, he had earned over 67 million in career income and had remained very active. His recent record of reaching 1,000 NHL games has only helped Kadri cement his image as one of the most valuable players of the Calgary Flames, both to the fans and analysts.In August 2022, Nazem Kadri signed a seven-year, 49 million deal with the Calgary Flames, making him stay with the team until the 2028-29 season. The agreement incorporates an average annual value (AAV) of 7 million, a base salary of 4.5 million in the 2025-26 season and a signing bonus of $2.5 million. There are also no-movement and modified no-trade clauses in the contract, which offer stability and flexibility in a career to Kadri.The NHL earnings have been very remarkable in terms of Kadri. By the 2025-26 season, he will have accumulated at least a little cash, a total of approximately 67,124,530 in salary , signing bonuses and other incentives. His financial growth is a sign of how successful he has been routinely on the ice, including a 35-goal season in 2024-25, his highest in his career so far. Kadri remains a top-six forward in the Calgary Flames, and he plays an important role as an offensive player not only in terms of goals and assists but also in terms of the overall offensive play.Besides his financial performance, Kadri has also garnered attention in recent on-ice performances. He remains a significant element of the Calgary Flames offensive, as in March 2026, his 1,000 th NHL game was celebrated. In the current times, people can expect him to play key roles in current NHL games, but analysts are monitoring his contributions in order to make a difference in the 2026 NHL playoffs. These reports of Kadri's performance and NHL news package, and the reports of his injuries, show that he remains in the league.In conclusion, the net worth and income of Nazem Kadri are an indication of his successful career in the NHL, which is both skillful, consistent and long-lasting. With the Calgary Flames still playing in the 2025- 26 season, Kadri is one of the most remarkable players on the ice and off the ice due to his input on the ice and his success in earning money.
Article Summary: Nazem kadri signed a seven-year, 49 million deal with the calgary flames in august 2022. By 2026, he had earned over 67 million in career income and had remained very active. His financial growth is a sign of how successful he has been routinely on the ice.

Article Title: Nintendo Indie World Showcase announced: Date, time, what to expect and how to watch
Article Text:
How and when to watch Nintendo Indie World Showcase



Pacific Time: 6:00 am

Eastern Time: 9:00 am

UK Time: 2:00 pm

Indian Standard Time: 7:30 pm

Indie World Showcase 3.3.2026

Join us on March 3 at 6am PT for a new Indie World Showcase! Tune in for roughly 15 minutes of news and updates on indie games coming to Nintendo Switch 2 and Nintendo Switch.

What to expected from Nintendo Indie World Showcase



Nintendo fans rejoice! You have a new date to circle on their calendars. A fresh Indie World Showcase has been officially announced for March 3, promising a burst of updates for the independent games heading to Nintendo platforms. According to a report, the presentation is expected to be a short one, clocking in at roughly 15 minutes. Moreover, this showcase is said to cover games for both the original Nintendo Switch and the newly released Nintendo Switch 2.This will give players on both systems a glimpse at what’s coming next to the eShop. Here’s all the details about the Nintendo Indie World Showcase and what to expect from it.The digital event will be streamed live on Nintendo’s official YouTube and Twitch channels. Since, it is a global broadcast, the start times vary by region:This is an Indie World Showcase and not a full-scale Nintendo Direct, which means you should not expect updates on major Nintendo-owned franchises like The Legend of Zelda or the long-rumored new 3D Mario project.Instead, the spotlight will strictly be “Nindies” – which are independent developers bringing their creative visions to Nintendo hardware, as per IGN India.Notably, the community is already looking forward to news about Hollow Knight: Silksong. The Hollow Knight recently received a high-profile "Enhanced" upgrade for the Switch 2 during last month’s Partner Showcase but the sequel remains one of the most anticipated titles.This showcase follows a massive February Partner Showcase wherein the company showcased Switch 2’s library including Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth and Bethesda’s Indiana Jones and the Great Circle.
Article Summary: The nintendo indie world showcase will take place on march 3 at 6am pt. The showcase is said to cover games for both the original nintendo switch and the newly released nintendo switch 2. The digital event will be streamed live on nintendo’s official youtube and twitch channels.

Article Title: Imperfect rivals look for the perfect night: India, England clash in third straight T20 World Cup semifinal
Article Text:
Suryakumar Yadav (R) with Tilak Varma during a practice session. (Getty Images)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

India arrive for final net session before T20 World Cup semifinal

MUMBAI: India and England will clash in their third consecutive T20 World Cup semifinal at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday night. Both sides have one win each in their previous knockout meetings, with the victor on both occasions going on to lift the trophy.England thrashed India by 10 wickets in the 2022 T20 World Cup semifinal, chasing 170 with openers Alex Hales (86 not out) and Jos Buttler (80 not out) dominating on a flat Adelaide pitch. Two years later in Providence, India avenged that defeat with a 68-run victory as spinners Kuldeep Yadav (3-19) and Axar Patel (3-28) bowled England out for 103 in 16.4 overs.India had beaten England 4-1 in a five-match T20I series last January, sealing it with a 150-run win at the Wankhede.However, the stakes are much higher in a World Cup semifinal.Both teams enter the match with confidence, though England appear to have greater momentum after winning all three of their Super 8 matches against Sri Lanka, Pakistan and New Zealand. India, meanwhile, will be looking to defy history — no defending champion has retained the T20 World Cup and no host nation has won the title. In fact, no defending champion has even reached the final in the subsequent edition.India topped Group A alongside Pakistan and associate teams, winning all four league matches. However, their batting has looked vulnerable on slower pitches against spin. That was evident during a collapse against the United States at Wankhede, where the hosts slumped to 77-6 before captain Suryakumar Yadav rescued them with an unbeaten 84 off 49 balls.England began their campaign with a narrow four-run win over Nepal at Wankhede, with Sam Curran defending 10 runs in the final over and Will Jacks producing a fine all-round effort.They later lost to the West Indies by 30 runs at the same venue, where Sherfane Rutherford smashed 76 not out off 42 balls and the Caribbean spinners stifled England’s batting.India’s Super 8 campaign started with a 76-run loss to South Africa in Ahmedabad. They bounced back by thrashing Zimbabwe by 72 runs in Chennai after posting 256 for four, and then beat the West Indies by five wickets at Eden Gardens, powered by Sanju Samson’s unbeaten 97 off 50 balls and Jasprit Bumrah’s double strike.England, meanwhile, began the Super 8s with a 51-run win over Sri Lanka, bowling them out for 95. They followed it up with a twowicket victory against Pakistan, highlighted by captain Harry Brook’s blistering 100 off 51 balls and Liam Dawson’s 3-24. England sealed a semifinal berth with a four-wicket win over New Zealand.Samson has been among India’s most consistent batters, in sharp contrast to opening partner Abhishek Sharma, who has managed just 80 runs in six matches at an average of 13.33, including three ducks.His fifty against Zimbabwe remains his only significant contribution. The left-hander will hope to rediscover his touch at a venue where he once smashed 135 off 54 balls.England too have concerns over their openers. Buttler has scored just 62 runs in seven matches at an average of 8.85, while Phil Salt has struggled for consistency apart from a 62 against Sri Lanka in Pallekele. Samson, who has struggled against short-pitched bowling, and the out-of-form Abhishek could face a stern test from Jofra Archer in the powerplay.However, Archer too has found Wankhede unforgiving, returning figures of 1-42 against Nepal and 1-48 against the West Indies.Ishan Kishan has been quiet after a match-winning 77 off 40 balls against Pakistan in Colombo. However, he, along with Surya, Tilak Varma and Hardik Pandya — all former Mumbai Indians players — will be familiar with the Wankhede conditions.Tilak looked more comfortable after moving from No. 3 to No. 6, scoring 44 not out off 16 balls against Zimbabwe and 27 off 15 against West Indies.India’s vulnerability against spin could again be tested by England’s attack comprising Adil Rashid, Liam Dawson, Rehan Ahmed and off-spinning allrounder Will Jacks. India will counter with Varun Chakravarthy and Axar Patel, though Varun has struggled for rhythm — a far cry from last year’s bilateral series against England when he claimed 14 wickets in five matches at an average of 9.85.
Article Summary: India and england will clash in their third consecutive t20 world cup semifinal. Both sides have one win each in their previous knockout meetings, with the victor on both occasions going on to lift the trophy. India had beaten england 4-1 in a five-match t20i series last january.

Article Title: Allen Finnishes off South Africa: Opener’s 33-ball ton sends New Zealand into final
Article Text:
Finn Allen (IANS Photo)

India players go BANG BANG in the nets, Hardik Pandya and others on fire at Wankhede

Jansen shines

KOLKATA: In one of the most one-sided matches in the T20 World Cup, New Zealand thrashed South Africa by nine wickets in the semifinal at the Eden Gardens here to make their second final. After the NZ spinners cast a spell to restrict South Africa to 169 for eight, their openers made a mockery of the SA attack to reach 173/1 in just 12.5 overs.SA bowlers were found running for cover as NZ openers Tim Seifert and Finn Allen tore them apart getting 117 runs by the 10th over.Both the players seemed to enjoy playing at a venue which will be their ‘home’ in a few days from now when they will join their new side Kolkata Knight Riders in the upcoming IPL.Although Seifert departed for 58 (33b, 4x7, 2x6), Allen got the fastest 100 in the World Cup with a six off just 33 balls.The opener got his first 50 in 19 balls and improved upon that by adding the next 50 in just 14 balls to seal the deal. Rachin Ravindra, another KKR batter, stayed unbeaten on 13 after doing the damage with the ball picking up two for 29.The strong South Africa batting line-up struggled to get going, tumbling to 77 for five in the first 11 overs after New Zealand skipper Mitchell Santner decided to bowl after winning the toss.Santner’s masterstroke in bringing Cole McConchie in the second over of the match proved fruitful as the off-spinner picked up left-handed Quinton de Kock and Ryan Rickelton off consecutive deliveries.The 34-year-old McConchie, who was added to the New Zealand squad as a travelling reserve after an injury to Michael Bracewell, had de Kock caught by Lockie Ferguson inside the circle and then accounted for Rickelton, caught by Allen at backward point.Things could have been worse for SA had Rachin Ravindra not dropped an easy catch of skipper Aiden Markram, then on 3, at midwicket off Lockie Ferguson in the fourth over.However, Ravindra, introduced as the sixth bowler in the eighth over of the match, made amends by having Markram caught by Daryl Mitchell inches above the ground at long-on.SA might have hoped for a change in fortunes when Glenn Phillips dropped David Miller on two at deep extra cover after positioning himself perfectly covering a good amount of ground. But Rachin had his man four balls later, this time Mitchell taking a skier easily at long-off.While McConchie ended with two wickets in his only over, Rachin claimed two for 29 in his four overs.Marco Jansen came good with the bat, stitching together a 73-run sixth wicket stand with Tristan Stubbs. The two did not hesitate to play some big shots as SA reached 100 losing five wickets in the 14th over, their slowest in this tournament.Ferguson, coming back for his second spell, finally got the breakthrough by having Stubbs bowled with a beautiful slower one. But Jansen counter-attacked hitting Ferguson for consecutive sixes to stay undefeated on a brave 55 off just 30 balls with the help of two fours and five sixes.
Article Summary: New zealand thrash south africa by nine wickets in t20 world cup semifinal. Nz openers tim seifert and finn allen tore them apart getting 117 runs by the 10th over. Rachin ravindra stayed unbeaten on 13 after doing the damage with the ball picking up two for 29.

Article Title: India vs England: Ahead of semis, Varun Chakravarthy puts in massive shift with Morne Morkel
Article Text:
Varun Chakaravarthy (Getty Images)

Why India vs England semi-final match will be a nightmare for bowlers | T20 World Cup 2026

MUMBAI: Wednesday’s nets session was an optional one, but Varun Chakravarthy was out there, alongside bowling coach Morne Morkel , slogging it out in searing heat.The focus throughout the session, and during the mammoth one that India put in on Tuesday evening, was on the length he needs to hit against England in the semifinal at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday.Varun started the T20 World Cup campaign with a bang, ending the four-match group stage with nine wickets at a strike rate of 8. But in the Super Eights, while bowling against stronger opponents, he was found somewhat wanting.In the three games, he took just two wickets at a strike-rate of 24 and conceded 10.16 runs per over, which is much higher than his career economy rate of 7.23. He finished with figures of 1/47 against South Africa, 1/35 against Zimbabwe and 1/40 against West Indies.Against South Africa, David Miller, Dewald Brevis and Tristan Stubbs showed the courage to take on the mystery spinner. As a result, he erred in his line and length—bowling a bit short or fuller when put under pressure—which brought him into the batters’ arc to hit him down the ground or either side of the wicket.The story repeated against the West Indies in the last match at the Eden Gardens, with Shimron Hetmyer and Jason Holder going after the Kolkata Knight Riders’ spinner. When the batters took him on, they curtailed his ability to apply the squeeze in the middle overs and take wickets to keep the opponents on the mat.Varun is still among the top-five wicket-takers in this edition with 12 scalps, but teams seem to have dissected his variations, especially the googly, which has been his go-to delivery to take wickets. Almost 75% of his wickets in T20Is have come with the googly. When he has bowled the googly at pace, rival batters have played him as an incoming bowler and tried to hit him straighter. When he has got one to turn away, batters have backed themselves and adjusted.With the likes of Phil Salt, Jos Buttler Harry Brook , Jacob Bethell and Will Jacks in their ranks, England will take a cue from Varun’s outings against South Africa and West Indies, make use of the shorter boundaries and put the 34-year-old under pressure.During the media interaction, Morkel revealed that discussions with Varun centred around getting “clarity” of his plans a day ahead of the match and “allowing him to walk away feeling good about his body”.“With the variations that Varun’s got, he’s got the ability to take a wicket with almost every ball. So, the discussion was that if he goes for a boundary, the aim is to move on to the next one and make sure he commits to that next ball. He’s hard to pick once you walk into the crease. So, for him it’s just about getting that confidence into the ball, getting his speed, his length and control right,” said Morkel.

He finished with figures of 1/47 against south africa, 1/35 against zimbabwe and 1/40 against west indies. The 34-year-old is still among the top-five wicket-takers in this edition with 12.
